verses.1: Then the LORD said to me, “Take a large scroll and write on it with an ordinary stylus: Maher-shalal-hash-baz.
verses.2: And I will appoint for Myself trustworthy witnesses—Uriah the priest and Zechariah son of Jeberekiah.”
verses.3: And I had relations with the prophetess, and she conceived and gave birth to a son. The LORD said to me, “Name him Maher-shalal-hash-baz.
verses.4: For before the boy knows how to cry ‘Father’ or ‘Mother,’ the wealth of Damascus and the plunder of Samaria will be carried off by the king of Assyria.”
verses.5: And the LORD spoke to me further:
verses.11: For this is what the LORD has spoken to me with a strong hand, instructing me not to walk in the way of this people:
verses.18: Here am I, and the children the LORD has given me as signs and symbols in Israel from the LORD of Hosts, who dwells on Mount Zion.
verses.19: When men tell you to consult mediums and spiritists who whisper and mutter, shouldn’t a people consult their God instead?Why consult the dead on behalf of the living?
verses.20: To the law and to the testimony! If they do not speak according to this word, they have no light of dawn.
verses.21: They will roam the land, dejected and hungry. When they are famished, they will become enraged; and looking upward, they will curse their king and their God.
verses.22: Then they will look to the earth and see only distress and darkness and the gloom of anguish. And they will be driven into utter darkness.
